    ArgumentCommand.getArguments()

  • Uses of CommandArgument in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.id

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.id that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    EntityTypeArgument
     
    class 
    IdentifiableArgument<I extends org.bukkit.Keyed>
    Gets a single identifiable object from the collection provided by IdentifiableArgument.getAll()
    class 
    MaterialArgument
    Gets a single Material from a single string argument
    class 
    TagArgument
    Gets a block group from a single string argument

  • Uses of CommandArgument in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.misc

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.misc that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    PluginArgument
    Gives the user a chose of plugins

  • Uses of CommandArgument in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.source

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.collection.source that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    PlayerArgument
    Provides a online user argument to the user
    class 
    UserArgument
    Provides a chose of OfflinePlayers to the user

  • Uses of CommandArgument in org.mose.command.arguments.operation

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.operation that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    AnyArgument<A>
    AnyArgument is used for if you have a collection of possible acceptable arguments with one of those arguments being passed to the command system
    class 
    ExactArgument
    The exact argument is used as a string argument where there is only one single possible argument.
    class 
    FlatRemainingArgument<T>
    The flat remaining argument is designed to get all the remaining arguments whereby the argument return a list.
    class 
    MappedArgumentWrapper<T,​J>
    Used for mapping one argument to another.
    class 
    OptionalArgument<T>
    The optional argument is designed to give the user a optional argument to give more detail, this could be that the user needs to specify a online player, however this could be optional if the user is already a player, whereby the optional value would be the user player.
    class 
    RemainingArgument<T>
    Parses all the string arguments that have not be processed after the last successful parse.
    class 
    SuggestionArgument<A>
    This allows you to give extra suggestions to a command argument.
    Methods in org.mose.command.arguments.operation that return CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NotNull CommandArgument<J>
    MappedArgumentWrapper.commandArgument()
    Returns the value of the commandArgument record component.
    @NotNull CommandArgument<T>
    OptionalArgument.getOriginalArgument()
    Gets the original argument to compare

    Description
     
    FlatRemainingArgument​(@NotNull String id, @NotNull CommandArgument<? extends Collection<T>>... argument)

    This one is mainly used with the MappedArgumentWrapper.

    Used for wrapping a single argument as a Remaining argument.

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.operation.permission that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    PermissionOrArgument<T>
    Allows to provide a better argument to the source if they have the provided permission

  • Uses of CommandArgument in org.mose.command.arguments.position

    Classes in org.mose.command.arguments.position that implement CommandArgument
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    BlockArgument
    BlockArgument provides a block location based upon the user input
    class 
    LocationArgument
    LocationArgument provides a exact location based upon the user input
    class 
    PositionArgument<N extends Number,​P>
    Common interface for all position based arguments (aka, x, y, z, world)
    class 
    WorldArgument
    Gets a world from a single argument, if the source of the command is locatable then this command becomes optional whereby if the string argument is not provided then it will use the world the source is in.
    Constructors in org.mose.command.arguments.position with parameters of type CommandArgument
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    PositionArgument​(@NotNull String id, @NotNull CommandArgument<N> argument)

    Class
    Description
    class 
    BooleanArgument
    Provides a boolean argument to the user.
    class 
    EnumArgument<E extends Enum<?>>
    Provides a chose of values from enum class

    Class
    Description
    class 
    DoubleArgument
    Gets a single double value from user input.
    class 
    IntegerArgument
    Gets a single int value from user input.

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    StringArgument
    Provides a single string argument (no spaces) to the user
    class 
    StringBetweenArgument
     
    class 
    StringCodeArguments
    Formats a command argument result with ChatColor codes
    Constructors in org.mose.command.arguments.simple.text with parameters of type CommandArgument
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    StringCodeArguments​(@NotNull CommandArgument<String> originalArgument)
    Allows all ChatColor values
     
    StringCodeArguments​(@NotNull CommandArgument<String> originalArgument, @NotNull Collection<org.bukkit.ChatColor> formats)
    Allows a specified set of ChatColor formats
     
    StringCodeArguments​(@NotNull CommandArgument<String> originalArgument, org.bukkit.ChatColor... formats)
    Allows a specified set of ChatColor formats
